item.title: This is my girl Miss Kitty and she passed away today.
item.description:
She had cancer, and she couldn't eat or breathe much anymore. I held her in my harms as she passed away. They said it was painless and she would just fall asleep. She is and always will be beautiful, and I told her this almost every day. I didn't want to take a picture of her like this at first, but I need to see her everyday and remind myself how beautiful she is.
She kinda always looked grumpy, but let me tell you, she was sweet and very kind.
You are a beautiful girl and I will never forget you.
item.dc:creator: moeber

item.title: Time for the unhinged XL Saturday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
vape cloud?
who am I, Liam Neeson?
i really need a vacation
not a fan of scarecrows.. that one scary book.. harold??
"banana cleaner"
not very topical but i liked it damnit
if only
another 'why didn't i think of that' business idea
mmmm ... washcloth nibble
pfffttt Karl Marx.. such a copier. Britney is looking a little rough though
mmm hmm
indeed
don't talk to me or my sons ever again
fr though getting the first real snowstorm of the year
it's not healthy to keep it inside
i don't know what this is from but i laughed bc the idea of a pastry being that expensive is quite comical!
that’s it for me.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Let us now celebrate a True American Hero! Eugene (a very) Goodman! Happy January 6
item.description: Eugene Goodman, the very definition of public servant, thank you for your service. Most Viral edit: first time for everything! Not a political person, just recognize a guy who went above and beyond to do the best job he could in a difficult situation.
item.dc:creator: mandalaffect

item.title: Karma can be classy sometimes.
item.description:
Hello Imgur. So Tuesday afternoon I got a surprise visitor. Last year I allowed a local food truck delivery driver to park his rig behind my house where I cannot do anything with unless I cut down trees. The nearest place to his house he can park charges $100 a week. Not to mention he lives down the road from me at a small apartment building so he cannot park there. The place I mentioned is also a 30 minute drive from his house. So we had the agreement he could park there under the condition he not damage the property or trees. He has kept his part of the promise and even went an extra step to pick up litter along the road next to where he parks. He come to visit to thank me for letting him park on my land. He brought a whole case of New York strip primals for me. I told him it was not necessary or expected but he told me that because he was allowed to park where he does he has been able to get deliveries done quicker and easier to the point he made a nice bonus and he saved enough that he could afford to buy the case for me without issue. I was very thankful to him for his more than generous gift as I was not expecting it nor even asked for any money for him to park. So we chatted a while and I told him I would feel better accepting such a large gift if he joined me and the wife for a big steak dinner sometime next week. So next Wednesday I’m going to be grilling some steaks. My only issue was that after processing I did not have room to store it in my freezer so me and the wife had to run to the store to buy a small freezer for it all. Won’t lie we buy meat in bulk and make use of a vacuum sealer to freeze it to preserve it long term. Purchased that sealer back in 2015 for 35 bucks with a coupon and it has more than payed for itself since. Got it all processed and packaged up and in the new freezer so it won’t go bad. We have enough to last us the entire year I think. Anyways remember being kind will always pay just do not expect it to give you primals although I won’t complain :). Have a great day everyone.
Ham wants a steak too. He watched me the entire time I was processing the primals drooling. Don’t worry he got some steak too.
item.dc:creator: retailmemedrone

item.title: Absolutely love this!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: How do skinny people survive winter?!
item.description: I made big changes to my life (sober from alcohol, walking every day, healthy eating, etc.) because you know, at 30 I shouldn’t have to be taking blood pressure meds and being winded by household stairs. Went from 270 to 220, blood pressure is entering a more healthy range, not winded by walking upstairs, feel more energetic, but the cold… nobody warned me about the cold. I either did not know or blissfully blocked it out in my head, but my threshold for dealing with the cold has gone down so much. Didn’t think much if at all into my skinnier friends being so cold all the time, but boy do I now understand a lot more. Glad I’m healthier than I have been, but man these winters are going to be harder.
item.dc:creator: CptChunk

item.title: Secret Santa gift?
item.description:
Received an unexpected letter in the mail today. My SS interests did include a line that charitable giving in lieu of gifts is appreciated so it would be the only explanation. I fully approve of this choice of charity.
To my SS: If this is from you, the postmark on the envelope says Dec 15 so I apologize for the delay. Not sure if this got lost in transit or ended up in a neighbor's mailbox and they only just now noticed. For those unfamiliar, the Satanic Temple is a civil rights organization. It doesn't believe in an actual Satan. It also shouldn't be confused with the Church of Satan.
item.dc:creator: IAmGodot

item.title: My living will has instructions to log into my accounts and let friends know if something happens to me.
item.description: I hate that some of my accounts have people on my friends list with "Last logged in 2+ years ago" You end up making friends and bonding over games and they just disappeared one day. They didn't say goodbye and you hope they just got busy with life but it can still worry you and hurt. I don't have to many friends I play with online these days but I don't ever want to have any of them look see my user name every few years and wonder what happened. (originally posted by @Sensiblyinteresting on 2023-01-08 02:49:28)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.dc:creator: aerrorfree

item.title: We tried a restaurant based on an Imgur post!
item.description:
@PiercedViking posted yesterday about a restaurant in Omaha that serves all kinds of deviled eggs. Being Omaha residents my mother, my son, and I knew we had to try it. Post: https://i.imgur.com/vG4hYep.jpeg
Mom tried traditional and BLTE, and my son had smoked salmon and buffalo blue.
I tried cheeseburger (fried) and gyro. Everything was really good and was well worth the 12 mile drive (Omaha is bigger than people realize). https://www.deviledeggco.com/
item.dc:creator: license2kilt

item.title: Happy Traitor's Day
item.description: FP Edit: Please vote against everyone who participated in, denied, or excused what happened on January 6, 2021. The way we move forward is to send a clear message that most of us don't want what the insurrectionists wanted on that day.

item.title: TheFreePizzaDude is back!!!
item.description:
Hi everyone, we are TheFreePizzaAngels Today we're going to send food to Imgurians in need, come join us!
If you'd like to help someone get food today, there are two pretty easy ways -
Any questions as an Angel? Just shoot us a message! We'll be on this username until 5pm central
You can also contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and we will send the food for you! https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude
If you need help today, here is how it works for requests -
It's much easier for us to help requests in the US but we'll do our best to pick up requests from any country. Putting your country in your comment helps us match you to an Angel in your area
Any questions as a requestor? Just leave a comment! Well have helpers on the post until 5pm central
12,564 meals since we started 793 grocery orders sent 6 more hours to help as many people as we can Let's get it going!!! ... Today's Pizza Tally 1118 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th by @OP 1159 5th, 6th, 7th and 8th by @OP 1205 9th and 10th by Pizza Angel @KezzySarus 1240 11th and 12th by Pizza Angel @NoRoutesFound 105 13th, 14th, 15th and 16th by Pizza Angel @IrvingSchwinglepuck 125 17th by Pizza Angel @HelaSisterOfThor 140 18th by Pizza Angel @IrvingSchwinglepuck 202 19th, 20th, 21st and 22nd by @OP 240 Michael Jordan by Pizza Angel @AyatollahBahloni 250 Kobe Bryant, 25th, 26th and 27th by @OP 255 28th and 29th by Pizza Angel @testudoaubrei 300 30th, 31st, 32nd and 33rd by @OP 301 34th and 35th by Pizza Angel @IrvingSvhwinglepuck 302 36th by Pizza Angel @enjt 310 37th & 38th by Pizza Angel @KezzySaurus 330 39th & 40th by Pizza Angel @AshtonWhitney 350 41st and The meaning of Life, the Universe, and everything by Pizza Angel @NoRoutesFound 405 45th, 46th, & 47th by Pizza Angel @MorganVPI 420 Blaze it 48th and 49th by Pizza Angel @invalidGiraffeDancer 425 50th by Pizza Angel @mgbhost 445 51st by Pizza Angel @boatdude87 500 52nd and 53rd by Pizza Angel @IrvingSvhwinglepuck 500 54th by Pizza Angel @Jyornoc 500 55th, 56th, 57th and 58th by @Op ... Alright everyone that's it for @OP Today we sent out 58 meals 3 straight weeks of 50+!!! That's 12,623 meals since we started 793 grocery orders sent Well done everyone, see you next week!
item.dc:creator: TheFreePizzaDude

item.title: Post thirty fourish of many in remembrance of my son, Sage Phoenix Corlett Waters, 2003-2023 + some memes he'dve liked
item.description:
My 20 year old son was killed on August 26 2023 in a car accident. The past few months have been rough, with his birthday coming up on February 3 I expect it to get rougher; tonight I woke up from a dream where I kept chasing after Sage, never quite catching him as he rounded corner after corner. I had this in my head: Time has been jammed since you left, Sage. Papa's hands empty, fists without purpose. Mama's heart drags, heavy with silence. Laughter’s a knife, twisting in the quiet you left. Days are just shadows, each one a dull ache. We’re adrift, lost in a half-light without end. Sleep tricks us, flashes your smile, then snatches it away. Your future—a mocking ghost, a bitter jest. Grief is a slow bleed, while love is a raw, unending burn. Dawns are cruel reruns, each as empty as the last. Grief screams of robbed tomorrows, relentless. Rooms echo with you, joy now just embers. Your absence is a shout in the stillness, a deafening void. It devours, leaves us clawing for air. Home is a hollow, ringing with the ghost of your laugh. Quiet is a liar, mimics your steps, your chuckle. Your gone-ness is a cliff we scale, each weary day. Grief is a thorn, love is the cut that keeps on bleeding.
Last night while in Choice Deli & Grocery, a local convenience store, when I heard the word salad of an excited teen with ADHD going over the pros and cons of the candy, taste, weight, price and more. Looking down the isle, I saw a young man, no more than seventeen, about the same height and build of a teenage Sage, with the same scruffy hair. Next to him, I saw his father with a shock of gray hair, one who looked slightly beleaguered, yet not annoyed, tired, but happy. "Dad, did you know that sour worms are sour because of the chemical on the outside? But I still like them better that the gummy bears. Dad, did you know gummy bears have gelatin in them? Oh look, this box of whoppers is a better per ounce deal, Dad." The father just sighed, smiled and waited as I spied on the interaction. His son rambled on. "I'm not really in the mood for whoppers, so I'll get sour worms. Dad did you see the box of whoppers was shaped like a house?," he asked, then taking the conversation on an 73 degree turn. "Dad, how old do you have to be to be a real estate agent? You know, when agents try to take you one way, you should go the opposite direction, Dad?" I grabbed the milk I was in the store for as the monologue went on, twisting and turning, few breaths being taken. As I stood in line behind the pair, I could feel the tears welling up from a fresh new memory of my son. I wanted to reach out to that father, tell him to remember this night, to cherish his son, but I stood mute, letting the pain wash over me. I have been through brush fires and forest fires, some even with Sage. I've weathered hurricanes and floods. I've lived in solitude in the Sahara, land of endless sun. So it came as no surprise when I got back in the car to fully embrace the tears that James Taylor reverberated in my head. "I've seen fire and I've seen rain. I've seen sunny days that I thought would never end. I've seen lonely times when I could not find a friend. But I always thought I'd see you one more time again." #RIP #Loss #Love #Grief #BuildingCharacter
item.dc:creator: earthwulf
